description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from vertical wooden staves held tightly together by two wide, metallic hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with subtle grain patterns, giving it an authentic, rustic appearance. The metallic bands are a matte gray, providing structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the wood. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and appear to be made from the same wood as the staves, with no visible openings or spouts. There are no handles or spigots, and the surface is free from any visible labels or markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas, often used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with two matte gray metal hoops, slightly bulging at the sides and flat on top and bottom. The surface is rustic and unmarked, with no handles or visible openings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, with both metal hoops and the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, highlighting the seamless connection of the staves and the uniformity of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Another angled view, showing a subtle variation in wood grain and the consistent placement of the metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with the same banding and wood texture visible.
